top_signals.title: India Turns to Russia, Brazil, and Venezuela for Crude
top_signals.summary: The world’s third-largest crude importer, India, is accelerating efforts to diversify its oil imports in the wake of the Middle East crisis. India had begun raising imports from Russia three years before the Iran war cut off most supplies from the Middle East, on which India relies for about half of its crude purchases. With the Strait of Hormuz de facto closed and only a handful of tankers moving out of the chokepoint per week, and few of these India-bound, the country is looking further away for sources of crude. Africa and South America…
top_signals.why_it_matters: Geopolitical escalation affecting commodities, shipping and cross-asset volatility.

top_signals.title: Japan 40-Year Bond Sale Sees Firmer Demand Than 12-Month Average
top_signals.summary: Japan’s 40-year government bond auction drew stronger demand than its 12-month average, as higher yields attracted investors despite inflation concerns driven by the Middle East conflict.
top_signals.why_it_matters: Geopolitical escalation affecting commodities, shipping and cross-asset volatility.

top_signals.title: Global Aluminum Rally Could Draw Record Exports From China
top_signals.summary: The rally in the global aluminum market could spur record outflows from China, where elevated prices are capping consumption.
top_signals.why_it_matters: Inflation expectations impacting rate path, bonds and global risk assets.

top_signals.title: Andy Briggs: UK is hurtling towards a pensions disaster
top_signals.summary: Without radical change, Britain will find itself amidst a pensions disaster, with millions worse off, writes Andy Briggs.

top_signals.title: Crude language is the least of BP’s Manifold problems
top_signals.summary: Yesterday saw the ousting of BP chair Albert Manifold after less than one year in the role. According to various media reports, Manifold was “shouty” and “aggressive”, with a “volcanic temper”. But try as it might to escape crude language, BP’s board may soon suffer similar missives from its shareholders after another c-suite scandal left [...]

top_signals.title: The Debate: Is Britain’s minimum wage too high?
top_signals.summary: Rishi Sunak has confessed minimum wage hikes he oversaw were too high. But is raising wages during a cost of living crisis really so bad?
